description: Demo of the SRD weapon kit added in US-128: Two-Weapon Fighting (a Light weapon in each hand), the Nick mastery (the off-hand strike rides free as part of the Attack action), and the Cleave mastery (a hit carries into a second adjacent enemy). A tight knot of goblins in the waystation hall gives the off-hand and the great-axe plenty to bite.
customIntroduction: The waystation hall is full of the wrong kind of noise — a goblin scavenger band, five strong, packed shoulder to shoulder over a split supply crate, snarling at each other and now at you. They're bunched so close a single good swing could open two of them at once. Your hands settle on your weapons. The question is only which ones.
customContext:
DEMO — WEAPON KIT (US-128): Two-Weapon Fighting, Nick, Cleave. The five goblins (faction monsters — three Minions, two Warriors) stand in a tight cluster in the great hall so the off-hand and the great-axe have adjacent targets to showcase.

SETUP — on encounter start, give the player a choice of loadouts via add_item, and explain each plainly in-fiction (the player equips through the inventory screen — main weapon to WEAPON, the second to OFF-HAND, or the two-hander to WEAPON):
  • add_item "scimitar" + add_item "dagger" (reason: "a matched pair for fighting with both hands") — a scimitar in the main hand and a dagger in the off-hand. BOTH are Light, so the player can make a bonus-action OFF-HAND strike after attacking. The dagger has the Nick mastery, so that off-hand strike costs NO Bonus Action — it folds into the Attack action for free.
  • add_item "greataxe" (reason: "a heavy two-hander") — has the Cleave mastery: when it hits a goblin, the blow carries into a SECOND goblin within 5 ft, once per turn, for weapon damage with no ability bonus.

WHAT TO DEMONSTRATE — narrate as the engine resolves them; do NOT fake the dice:
1. TWO-WEAPON FIGHTING: with a Light weapon in each hand, after the player's ATTACK the OFF-HAND button lights up — a second strike with the dagger. Its damage adds NO ability modifier (the roll still does).
2. NICK: because the dagger has Nick, that off-hand strike is FREE (part of the Attack action), so the Bonus Action stays open for something else. Point this out — Nick is the difference between 'costs your Bonus Action' and 'free'.
3. CLEAVE: with the greataxe, a hit on one packed goblin spills into an adjacent second goblin automatically — once per turn. The cluster is arranged so this always has a target.
4. (Rules note, not shown: a Loading weapon like a crossbow fires only once per Action even with Extra Attack — relevant at higher levels.)

Let the player re-equip mid-fight to feel both styles: dual-wield to clear minions with two strikes a turn, or swing the great-axe to cleave the cluster. The goblins fight normally (Nimble Escape, pack tactics); there's no objective beyond the lesson — dropping the band completes it.
objective: Arm up — twin blades or a great-axe — and cut down the goblin knot. Feel how the off-hand, Nick, and Cleave each change the math of a turn.

then.message: DEMO: the GM will hand you a SCIMITAR + DAGGER (dual-wield) and a GREATAXE. Equip the scimitar to WEAPON and the dagger to OFF-HAND, then ATTACK — the OFF-HAND button makes a second strike (no ability bonus to its damage), and because the dagger has Nick it's FREE (no Bonus Action). Or wield the greataxe: a hit CLEAVES into a second adjacent goblin. The goblins are packed tight so both shine.
